title: 92% of CFOs, top finance staff feel pressure to show ROI from AI: survey
excerpt: Half of survey respondents said their AI agents have yielded only limited measurable ROI, according to Avalara.
content_html:
Jim Tyson

Tue, July 21, 2026 at 8:34 AM MST 2 min read

This story was originally published on CFO Dive. To receive daily news and insights, subscribe to our free daily CFO Dive newsletter.

Dive Brief:

  • Ninety-two percent of CFOs and top finance executives feel pressure to show that investment in artificial intelligence yields a decent return, and only 7% say their organization focuses more on AI governance than speed of adoption, Avalara discovered through a survey.

  • Forty-four percent of respondents are only somewhat confident they could explain the actions of an AI agent to an auditor or regulator, and 76% say their organizations lack the in-house expertise sufficient to understand how their AI operates, Avalara, a provider of AI tax compliance software, said Tuesday.

  • "Nearly every leader we surveyed feels personal pressure to prove these investments are delivering," Avalara Executive Vice President Jayme Fishman said in a statement. "Meanwhile, the controls, expertise and accountability meant to sit underneath them are still being built."

Dive Insight:

Worldwide spending on AI will surge 47% this year to $2.6 trillion from $1.76 trillion in 2025, according to Gartner, exceeding the gross domestic product of both Canada and Australia. By 2030, AI investment will rise 120% more to $5.62 trillion.

The rush to adopt AI thrusts CFOs into a dilemma: They must fulfill their role as the main line of defense against wasteful spending while facing institutional, investor and competitive pressure to fund AI projects that, on their face, promise big returns.

"What stood out to me is the tension finance leaders are managing every day," Avalara CEO Hugo Sarrazin said in a statement. "They're being asked to move quickly, prove value and modernize critical processes while also protecting the controls and governance their businesses depend on."

In one sign that AI oversight lags adoption, 30% of survey respondents said their company has not updated internal controls within the last year, Avalara found in its survey of 1,505 CFOs and senior finance executives.

Half of survey respondents said their AI agents have yielded only limited measurable ROI, Avalara said.

Accountability for AI errors is also spotty, with 23% of respondents saying responsibility for a mishap would fall to no one or would be unclear, Avalara said.

Also, 46% of those surveyed said their AI incident response plans are untested or still in development, and only 28% require documented audit logs showing how their AI agent reaches its decisions, according to Avalara.

Companies should build and operate AI agents using verified, domain specific data and ensure transparency in decision-making, Sarrazin said.

sentences.en: Companies should build and operate AI agents using verified, domain specific data and ensure transparency in decision-making, Sarrazin said.
sentences.zh: 扎拉青说，公司应该使用经过验证的特定领域数据来构建和运营人工智能代理，并确保决策的透明度。

content_html_zh:
吉姆·泰森

2026 年 7 月 21 日星期二上午 8:34（MST） 阅读 2 分钟

本文最初发表于 CFO Dive。 要接收每日新闻和见解，请订阅我们免费的每日 CFO Dive 时事通讯。

潜水简介：

  • 阿瓦拉拉通过一项调查发现，92% 的首席财务官和高级财务主管感到压力，需要证明对人工智能的投资会产生可观的回报，只有 7% 的人表示他们的组织更关注人工智能治理而不是采用速度。
  • 人工智能税务合规软件提供商 Avalara 周二表示，44% 的受访者对自己能够向审计师或监管机构解释人工智能代理的行为只有一定程度的信心，76% 的受访者表示，他们的组织缺乏足够的内部专业知识来了解人工智能的运作方式。
  • 阿瓦拉拉执行副总裁杰梅·菲什曼 (Jayme Fishman) 在一份声明中表示：“我们调查的几乎每位领导者都感受到了证明这些投资正在发挥作用的个人压力。” “与此同时，其背后的控制、专业知识和责任仍在建设中。”

潜水洞察力：

据 Gartner 称，今年全球人工智能支出将从 2025 年的 1.76 万亿美元飙升 47% 至 2.6 万亿美元，超过加拿大和澳大利亚的国内生产总值。 到 2030 年，人工智能投资将增长 120%，达到 5.62 万亿美元。

急于采用人工智能让首席财务官陷入了两难境地：他们必须履行自己作为防止浪费支出的主线的角色，同时面临机构、投资者和竞争压力，为表面上承诺丰厚回报的人工智能项目提供资金。

Avalara 首席执行官 Hugo Sarrazin 在一份声明中表示：“令我印象深刻的是，财务领导人每天都在应对紧张的局势。” “他们被要求迅速采取行动，证明价值并使关键流程现代化，同时保护其业务所依赖的控制和治理。”

Avalara 在对 1,505 名首席财务官和高级财务主管进行的调查中发现，30% 的调查受访者表示，他们的公司在去年没有更新内部控制，这是人工智能监督滞后于采用的一个迹象。

阿瓦拉拉表示，一半的受访者表示，他们的人工智能代理仅产生了有限的可衡量投资回报率。

阿瓦拉拉表示，人工智能错误的责任也参差不齐，23% 的受访者表示，事故责任不会落到任何人身上，或者责任不明确。

此外，Avalara 表示，46% 的受访者表示，他们的人工智能事件响应计划尚未经过测试或仍在开发中，只有 28% 的受访者需要记录审计日志，显示他们的人工智能代理如何做出决策。

扎拉青说，公司应该使用经过验证的特定领域数据来构建和运营人工智能代理，并确保决策的透明度。
